calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Set For Trial On Jun-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgement Pursuant To Ccp 473 (B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendants Motion, Declaration Of Andrew Quan; Exhibit A; [Proposed] Order; [Proposed] Answer
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ASFAW TEDLA's Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgement Pursuant To Ccp 473 (B).

Defendant Asfaw Tedla's motion to set aside default and vacate default judgement pursuant to CCP 473(b) is granted. Since any doubts as to the application of section 437 are resolved in favor of the party seeking relief, the court finds that Mr. Tesla has shown that the entry of default and default judgement were taken against him because of his or his counsel's mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect due to the understanding of Mr. Tedla's counsel's that entry of default will not be sought by plaintiff before notice is given to Mr. Tedla's counsel. (See Shapiro v. Clark (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 1128, 1139, (" Because the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, 'any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default'")). The default and default judgment shall be vacated and defendant shall file his proposed answer to the complaint within five days of the entry of this order.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/NL)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Off Calendar
